quote_of_day.text:
Like I saw a video of a goddamned salamander spitting fire. It looked absolutely real and like accidental footage.

When the lows are well behaved the simulation starts glitching towards them. Otherwise you get boring pablum.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A high pressure system of caste animosity dominated the board, with a front of anti-pajeet sentiment blowing in from the west.

Anon criticized pajeet critical thinking, sparking a scattered but intense discussion.

A low-pressure trough of anti-Bhimrashtra sentiment moved in, with one anon boasting of harming the region through littering, fuel waste, and buying foreign goods.

A light drizzle of personal sharing also fell across the board, as one anon asked about others' dreams and goals, receiving mixed responses.

The conversation was overcast with a chance of relationship and marriage discussions.

articles.title: Indiachan's East of Inch Newspaper Redirected After Indus Shutdown
articles.body:
Anon introduced the East of Inch newspaper, previously run by the IAC group on the now-shut Indus platform, and provided links to the main site and the newspaper on Indiachan.

The newspaper contains topics shared on the board, with 61 issues archived so far. One anon expressed appreciation for the OP's effort, calling it 'one of the best ideas' they've seen, and shared their enjoyment of the radio content.

The OP had considered creating a full-fledged TTS-type radio with programming but was deterred by the scope and limited user base. Another anon suggested the OP create their own imageboard, similar to Induschan, citing their creativity and talent.

The OP was also asked about the possibility of a 'clean, filtered version' of a voiceover or podcast-like media, which they seemed open to exploring.

Anon discussed the potential for a database or podcast-like system, pulling Indiachan threads and formatting them for a radio broadcast with a TTS speaker. They noted this would be doable but might sound robotic.

The conversation also touched on the idea of creating a new imageboard, with one anon stating they'd make significant changes, including rules, if they were to start one. Others expressed interest in such a platform, citing the current imageboard's limitations.

Anon also teased the possibility of a more ambitious project, a working radio with Indiachan news and talk shows, suggesting a new thread would be created if it seemed feasible.

One respondent warned the OP about becoming an 'oversensitive censoring overlord,' reflecting a common concern about imageboards and free speech.

articles.title: Anon Criticizes Pajeet Critical Thinking
articles.body:
Anon expressed frustration with what they perceive as a deficiency in critical thinking among some Indian individuals, suggesting they blindly accept their ancestors' claims to boost legitimacy.

Another anon responded by criticizing Islam, stating its followers are responsible for filthy and disgusting conditions in various areas. A respondent brought up the topic of ancient civilizations, mentioning Lemuria, Atlantis, and Shambhala as real places, and suggesting that multiple gods, including YHWH, existed.

Anon also referenced a video of a salamander spitting fire, joking that when low points are well-behaved, the simulation starts to glitch. One participant dismissed the simulation theory, labeling its adherents as "smooth brains" easily brainwashed by figures like Tate.

Anon compared Indian religions to Abrahamic faiths, suggesting the former are more interesting, albeit more amoral. A respondent claimed their ancestor fought in World War I as part of the British-Indian Army.

Anon defended the historical accuracy of the Ramayan and Mahabharat, while another participant criticized biblical and Islamic mythology. A poster joked about the perceived stupidity of average pajeet, while another boasted about their supposed ancestor's accomplishments.

articles.title: Anon Boasts of Harming Bhimrashtra
articles.body:
Anon posted a declaration of intentionally causing harm to Bhimrashtra, claiming to litter, waste fuel, buy foreign goods, provide content to Wignats, and generally act against the nation's interests.

Another anon responded by pointing out that India already faces significant issues such as being a garbage dump, fuel shortages, and that buying foreign goods only benefits tax collection and bureaucrats.

A different anon expressed a desire to see Bhimrashtra suffer, stating they would laugh at the sight of dead bodies of 'dasyu rapists' and envision their land being free again.

In response, an anon labeled someone a 'dalit' and made an insult about their social status. One user joked about wanting Bhimrashtra to be humiliated by foreigners, suggesting that dalits have contributed to India's negative reputation.

Anon also debated about the motivations of others, with one stating they act solely in their own self-interest, and another suggesting that 'trve UCs' do not care about others.

Someone asked why another user cares about the opinion of foreigners, questioning if they are of a lower caste.

The conversation also involved personal attacks and insults, with one user being told to leave the country if they hate it so much, and another responding that they are 'doing their best' to harm Bhimrashtra.

articles.title: Anon shares personal dreams, gets mixed responses
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion by sharing their dream of having two wives.

Another anon pointed out that in Islam, a man can have up to four wives if he treats them equally, and shared a news article on the topic.

This anon also joked about their own goal of getting a job and a wife. One respondent expressed a desire for two women to be devoted to them for sex.

Others shared their own dreams, including building rental houses, having multiple wives from different regions, and becoming an international war criminal. Some anons offered supportive comments, while others made jokes or vulgar remarks.
